title: VM not starting
body:
### Issue Description

% podman ps
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:53109: connect: connection refused

% podman info
OS: darwin/amd64
buildOrigin: pkginstaller
provider: applehv
version: 5.5.1

% podman machine list
NAME                    VM TYPE     CREATED        LAST UP     CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default  applehv     5 minutes ago  Never       4           3.725GiB    93GiB


Stucked on this step:
 % podman machine start --log-level=debug
.
.
.
INFO[0000] waiting for VM to stop                       
[GIN] 2025/07/13 - 13:11:38 | 200 |    3.255308ms |       127.0.0.1 | GET      "/vm/state"
[GIN] 2025/07/13 - 13:11:38 | 200 |    3.756915ms |       127.0.0.1 | GET      "/vm/state"
[GIN] 2025/07/13 - 13:11:43 | 200 |      45.624µs |       127.0.0.1 | GET      "/vm/state"
[GIN] 2025/07/13 - 13:11:43 | 200 |      57.474µs |       127.0.0.1 | GET      "/vm/state"
[GIN] 2025/07/13 - 13:11:48 | 200 |       50.91µs |       127.0.0.1 | GET      "/vm/state"
[GIN] 2025/07/13 - 13:11:48 | 200 |      84.801µs |       127.0.0.1 | GET      "/vm/state"
[GIN] 2025/07/13 - 13:11:53 | 200 |      90.286µs |       127.0.0.1 | GET      "/vm/state"
[GIN] 2025/07/13 - 13:11:54 | 200 |      43.526µs |       127.0.0.1 | GET      "/vm/state"
[GIN] 2025/07/13 - 13:11:59 | 200 |       48.08µs |       127.0.0.1 | GET      "/vm/state"
[GIN] 2025/07/13 - 13:11:59 | 200 |     138.876µs |       127.0.0.1 | GET      "/vm/state"

comments.body: Does this still occur if you restart the system, or create a new `podman machine` VM?

> Does this still occur if you restart the system, or create a new `podman machine` VM?

Yes, same issue. Already reinstalled podman and restarted the system. Do you have any other suggestion?

running podman machine init without loglevel:

% podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

It stays on this status forever
@ashley-cui Anything more we can do for debug?
Does it still happen after you do a `podman machine reset`? Also after it hangs, in a new terminal, try `ps aux | grep vfkit`, is vfkit running?

SOLVED!!

I updated the MacOS to the last version and it worked. The issue was the VM starting forever, probably it was something related with the mac hypervisor
